title: heracleum sphondylium subsp. alpinum
intro: Heracleum sphondylium subsp. alpinum, also known as wild angelica or alpine hogweed, is a biennial plant that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae). This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and ease digestive issues, and is used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis, gout, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Heracleum sphondylium subsp. alpinum include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to protect against cell damage and reduce the risk of chronic diseases. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
